channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Military Advisor Warns US Blockade "Will Fail," Threatens Confrontation
item.description: A top military advisor to Iran's supreme leader declared that the US port blockade will fail and warned of confrontation if it continues, as diplomatic talks remain deadlocked.

item.title: 🇰🇷🇺🇸⚓ South Korea to Lead Joint Maritime Drill With US for First Time
item.description: South Korea will lead multinational naval forces for the first time in a US-led maritime exercise starting in June, marking a significant step in defense cooperation amid regional tensions.

item.title: 💰🇺🇸⚔️ Iran War Cost Hits $25 Billion as Hegseth Defends Record Pentagon Budget
item.description: The US military campaign against Iran has cost $25 billion so far, the Pentagon disclosed to Congress as Defense Secretary Hegseth defended the record defense budget amid Democratic criticism.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇮🇳🚢 Japan Shares Guarded Mogami Warship Design With India in Defense Boost
item.description: Tokyo has offered Delhi the plans to its $500-million Mogami-class warship so that it can be built in India using local industry, marking a major defense technology transfer between the two nations.

item.title: 📰📉🌍 World Press Freedom Declines as Authoritarianism Rises Globally
item.description: Three in four countries now have press freedom rated "problematic" or worse, with the 2026 World Press Freedom Index painting a bleak picture as authoritarian governance expands worldwide.

item.title: 🚢 US seeks international coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved the creation of the Maritime Freedom Construct (MFC), a joint State Department-Pentagon initiative to restore freedom of navigation. The move follows months of disrupted shipping as Iran blockaded the strait in response to US-Israeli attacks.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor Merz says relations with Trump remain good despite Iran spat
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz downplayed tensions with Washington over Germany not sending naval vessels to Hormuz, while warning of economic fallout from the Iran conflict. Trump had criticized Germany and NATO allies for not supporting the maritime operation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Bank of England expected to hold rates as Iran war clouds economic outlook
item.description: The UK central bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged, awaiting clearer signals on the economic impact of the Iran conflict. Markets are watching for any hints of future rate moves amid oil-driven inflation pressures.

item.title: 🛢️ Brent crude surges past on stalled Iran talks
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 6% as negotiations between Washington and Tehran showed no signs of progress. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, with analysts warning prices could climb further if the conflict escalates.

item.title: 🌍 Countries conclude first fossil fuel phase-out conference in Colombia
item.description: Delegates from 56 nations wrapped up the Santa Marta conference on transitioning away from fossil fuels. No binding commitments emerged, but participants highlighted financing gaps for developing nations and announced the next meeting will be in Tuvalu.

item.title: ⚖️ UN Reports Iran Executed 21 and Arrested 4,000 Since War Began
item.description: Iran has executed at least 21 people and arrested over 4,000 on national security charges since the US-Israel conflict began, according to UN human rights chief Volker Turk.

item.title: 💰 US War in Iran Has Cost $25 Billion So Far, Pentagon Official Says
item.description: A senior Pentagon official provided the first official estimate of the military price tag for the Iran conflict, an amount equal to NASA's entire annual budget.

item.title: ⚔️ US Military Commander to Brief Trump on New Options Against Iran
item.description: President Trump will receive a briefing from CENTCOM leader Brad Cooper on new plans for potential military action against Iran as negotiations remain stalled.

item.title: 🕊️ Trump Discusses Ukraine Ceasefire With Putin, Suggests Progress
item.description: President Trump said he discussed a possible ceasefire in the Ukraine war with Putin and hinted the Russian leader could agree to a temporary truce.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Seeks Sports Comeback Despite Ukraine War, Sparking Anger in Kyiv
item.description: Russia is pushing to return to international sports competitions, including curling, drawing sharp criticism from Ukrainian officials who see it as normalizing the ongoing invasion. A key meeting on Russian sports participation is upcoming.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Pentagon Releases M in Ukraine Aid After Congressional Pressure
item.description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announced the release of stalled Ukraine aid funds after lawmakers, including Senator McConnell, criticized that the money was "collecting dust" at the Pentagon while Kyiv fought Russian forces.

item.title: 🛰️ US and Allies Race to Defend Satellites From Russian Space Threats
item.description: Evidence that Russia is developing nuclear-armed spacecraft to target Western satellites has sparked urgent efforts by the US and allies to develop defensive capabilities in orbit, raising concerns about the militarization of space.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Shifts to Daily Drone Strikes Against Israeli Troops in Lebanon
item.description: The Iran-backed group is transitioning from heavy artillery fire to precision drone attacks on Israeli forces in southern Lebanon, marking a tactical shift in the ongoing border conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia Scales Down Victory Day Parade Citing Ukrainian Attack Threat
item.description: The Kremlin announced a significantly reduced May 9 Victory Day parade without the usual display of military hardware, citing increased threat of Ukrainian attacks on Moscow.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Surge as US-Iran Deadlock Keeps Gulf Supply Offline
item.description: Brent crude extended gains to near $120/barrel as the US-Iran standoff continues to block crucial Middle East oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, with no resolution in sight.

item.title: 🏛️ Powell to Remain on Fed Board, Citing Unprecedented Trump Administration Attacks
item.description: Fed Chair Jerome Powell announced he will stay on as a Federal Reserve governor after his chairmanship ends next month, defending the central bank independence amid what he called unprecedented legal battering by the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Trump Reviews Potential US Troop Reduction in Germany
item.description: President Trump said his administration is studying a reduction of US forces in Germany, citing Berlin failure to send naval assets to help open the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Poised to Raise Oil Output Despite UAE Exit and Iran War
item.description: OPEC+ is expected to agree to a small production increase at its Sunday meeting even as the Iran conflict blocks Gulf exports and the UAE prepares to exit the cartel on May 1.

item.title: ☢️ UN Nuclear Test Ban Chief Warns US and Russia Against Resuming Tests
item.description: The head of the Comprehensive Nuclear Test Ban Treaty Organization warned that if the US or Russia conducts a nuclear test, other nations will follow, potentially starting an unstoppable spiral of proliferation.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine House committee advances impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: A congressional justice committee ruled there was "probable cause" to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte over allegations of unexplained wealth, misuse of state funds, and threats to assassinate the president. The case now moves to the full House.
